title: Code-like content speed
description: Variable names, methods, brackets — coder rhythm
text: function getUserById(userId) { const user = await db.users.findOne({ id: userId }); if (!user) throw new NotFoundError('user'); return user.toJSON(); } export default getUserById; // also: validateInput, checkPermission, logAccess

tips.text: Code is English with stricter punctuation. The words you know; the brackets you drill.
Let the braces and parentheses fall like commas - part of the line, not a stumble in it.

tips.target: function getUserById(userId) { const user = await db.users.findOne({ id: userId }); if (!user)

tips.text: Engineers live in this notation for hours at a stretch. Make the symbols automatic and the work gets quieter.

tips.target: throw new NotFoundError('user'); return user.toJSON(); } export default getUserById; // also: validateInput, checkPermission, logAccess

character_tips.anna: Code is structured English. Drill the symbols, and the rest is just words you already trust.
character_tips.maxim: The brackets are where careless typists lose minutes. Place each one without looking and you keep the thread.
character_tips.knopych: Treat braces and semicolons as old friends, not surprises. They sit in the same spot every time.
character_tips.klavochka: The language of machines rewards exactness. Be kind to the line, but be precise. - Eleanor Hayes, editor
